pi_items.verified_value: Target (official title: 'Practice addition with a target game')

pi_items.source: GitHub resource directory https://github.com/gcompris/GCompris-qt/tree/master/src/activities/target/resource shows 5 numbered level directories (1, 2, 3, 4, 5). ActivityInfo.qml confirms '5 progressive difficulty levels (1-5)'.
verifier.py line 124: 'passed = (score >= 50 and vlm_data.get('activity_found') and (vlm_data.get('gameplay_detected') or vlm_data.get('progression_observed')))'

privileged_info_summary: The Target activity is a real GCompris educational game focused on practicing addition through dart-throwing gameplay. The activity has exactly 5 levels available (verified via GitHub resource directory structure and ActivityInfo metadata). The task requires completing at least 3 levels, which is achievable given the 5 available levels. The pass threshold of 50 points is correctly implemented in the verifier, which awards points for: finding the activity (20 pts), gameplay interaction (30 pts), level progression (30 pts), application state modification (10 pts), and returning to menu (10 pts). The data is not synthetic; the task uses the standard GCompris-qt package from Ubuntu/Debian repositories.
